title: Network Security, Attacks, Segmentation, and Hardening · Incident Case: Net Layer2 Attacks
summary: Threat-model and harden enterprise, guest, IoT, OT, management, and remote-access network zones. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net layer2 attacks increment in a defense-in-depth network security plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain VLAN hopping, MAC flooding, ARP poisoning or spoofing, rogue DHCP, and their switching impact and evidence.
Enforce trusted, untrusted, screened, guest, BYOD, IoT, IIoT, SCADA, ICS, and OT boundaries with explicit flows.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
